summary: Keep the local ad-hoc app identity stable across rebuilds so macOS Accessibility approval no longer points at a stale CDHash.
scope: Sign the app with an explicit designated requirement based on com.huyee.gesture-control.prototype.
Reset only the stale GestureControl Accessibility record once and register the final installed app again.
Keep all gesture recognition and system-control behavior from 0.4.0 unchanged.
validation: Release app build and strict codesign verification passed
Targeted tccutil reset completed without changing other applications
notes: The user must turn on the newly registered GestureControl switch once; macOS does not allow an app to grant this security permission to itself.
This remains a local prototype signature. Developer ID signing and notarization are still required for external distribution.
